And then the other thing is what the book doesn't come with are, you know, scripts. Correct. So the lady asked, how different is selling to the 35 and under group who grew up with high tech texting and things like that? Either of you? You know, I don't know if there's that much of a difference. Certainly maybe their access to different media. I don't know if I know it's a question that is that we're trying to sell via text because I don't think that's going to work. No, no. And by the way, that's not what this program is about. This program is about one-on-one selling. Right. Right. So the Google slap is valid. Yeah. It is. More of a problem. Right. Right. So their own attention span is more of a problem. Right. There's a bunch of new research. I set it aside to use, but a bunch of new research came out again this week. It's actually damaging IQ, the ability to do critical thinking, all of that. So I think that's different. Structurally, no difference. Selling a insurance policy to a 30-year-old, the reasons for buying are different. The method of buying and the method of selling, ultimately you're going to have to get face to face. You got to get mom and dad at the kitchen table. You got to close that sale. I think that also, if you are 35 selling to that market, you will make assumptions based on your own behavior that are not at all universally true. So one of the biggest problems now in business to business with sales forces, I have a client right now where we're about to clean house, is the salespeople insist on just emailing the proposals. And everybody's confused about why they're not closing. And even to a 30-year-old buyer at the other end, that's not going to close a sale. I'm sorry, it's not going to do it. And so I think people look for, they expect, they believe in these differences by age, by gender, by ethnicity, that are really nuanced differences, not significant differences. It's not structural differences. The sales are going to remain the same. We talked about that. There was an article in the Wall Street Journal about a woman that owned a business that had all millennium sellers. And she had to stop them from sending emails and make phone calls. Sales went up 25% the first month and dramatically since then. So there's a bias to think, well, all everybody wants to do is send emails out and you can get an auto responder to that. Well, one of the easiest things, I shouldn't say easiest, but one of the best things, if someone's watching this, wants to improve their business, is to have a salesperson. Absolutely no question. If you don't have one, it's one of the fastest ways to make way more money. And just with that strategy right there, hey, we're not going to send emails anymore. We're actually going to talk to a human being. And I think it might even work better with the 25-year-old who's not used to it at all. So is there a question up here? You had questions and now I don't see any questions. Okay, Eric, I guess he's talking about this script book here and I don't even know the answers I'm going to ask you. Do you have scripts for following up on market materials? Absolutely. You're going to make reference to the market materials. You're going to talk a little bit about that. Put it in place in your business. It is the fastest way for you to increase your profits without spending another penny on marketing. Nick, if they've got salespeople, how should they use this? This is your training program. So you're going to need to modify your scripts, obviously, to fit your business model and fit your business offering. But this is your training model. So you want to scale up your sales force because they won their cost. You want to move to day two where they're a revenue item for you. You want to scale them up the fastest way. This is the fastest way to do it. And again, the reason this is such a powerful sales training course is because it's also working on their head and the psychology and the reasoning behind why you're doing what you're doing. Because if you just tell them, oh, you've got to, here, I'm going to give you these scripts and you've got to memorize these scripts, there's going to be that pushback. But if they listen to the whole section on why you need to script your presentation, they're going to get it. They're going to make a lot of sense to them. And no one's going to say, well, I don't want to be a professional and let's make as much money as I can make. So take advantage of this. Get this right now. Nick, the fashion play to GKIC.
